channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Has Bitcoin become too dependent on ETFs and macro news? — 104 replies — 285.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Has Bitcoin become too dependent on ETFs and macro news?

Open thread • Replies: 104 • Updated: 2026-07-13 21:02 UTC

OP

Seems like every BTC pump or dump is blamed on ETF flows or macro headlines.

>Is that just the reality now, or are people overlooking other factors?

Early replies (first 3)

62450753
>>62450728
Its 100% cz dependent, just tradingview has to write something to the price action.

62450760
>>62450728
Btc does this thing where it lulls the newfag into a false sense of superiority. Both in bull and bear markets. In a bull market, it tricks newfags into thinking it will go up forever and traps them in overleveraged longs and in bear markets, it tricks them into it never going back up and they either sell low, sell covered calls or lever up short and get milked. Just hold spot and don't pay attention for a decade.

62450902
>>62450753
cz?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62464251
>>62464121
You know what? That's fair. I relied too much on 90s sitcom tropes. It should just end episodically, there's just no more seasons and that's it.
>>62464142
I don't have much going on in my life and my family and friends find my occurrences boring. And it's supposed to be a comedy. What drama? I want it to be goofy and for all publics!

62464760
>>62464033
Take stablecoins for another example. Zero. Zilch. Nada. $0.00 -- Remember when 100% of USDT used to be on Bitcoin? Now it's 0%, Omni is dead and Tether has moved to every other chain. There is nothing more to Bitcoin than BTC the asset.

62464820
I have a credit card that gives me cash back in the form of BTC. So my investment strategy is just skimming off my purchases anyway.

62465005
>>62464251
Why do you think you're right about that and you think you're so sure about it?

62465089
>>62465005
I don't understand the question. Right about what.

62466102
>>62464115
Cool. There's clearly more happening around bitcoin now than there was a few years ago.

62466109
>>62464760
I'm talking about native BTC, not stablecoins. Those are two different discussions.

62466120
Imagine having to pay for each website you visited…that’s how stupid it is for proof of work, btc will never work long term (already sucks now)

62466195
>>62459656
Well this is a link board so I won't say anything negative. I hope link goes to 10k eoy for all the anons holding it.

62466244
>>62466102
You do realize Aave is on Ethereum, right? It's great for BTC the asset but not so great for Bitcoin the network. When you're saying innovation isn't happening on Bitcoin base layer but "around it", do you mean on Aave for example?
>>62466109
And I was talking about innovation on Bitcoin in general. Originally I replied to
>why are people still building new infrastructure around it?
saying there's no infrastructure, well... besides bridging BTC to Ethereum. Saying there's no stablecoin infra on top of Bitcoin is relevant to the discussion.

62466717
>>62466244
Bridging is risky

62466757
>>62466195
Chinlink

62467179
>>62466120
What drugs you're taking ser?

62467263
>>62466195
buy link

62467380
>>62463301
Is this real

62468734
>>62463320
I remember this .how time flies

62469658
>Icp

62469661
>>62464820
How's that possible?

62470315
>>62469658
I don't know any reason to sell

62471740
>>62463265
Whales = me

62472334
>>62463285
No way. You don't understand what Bitcoin is

62472669
>>62466717
Why would you risk it

62472729
>>62450728
is this the COME AND FILE YO TAXES guy?

62473419
>>62472729
be clear anon

62473816
>>62469661
Coinbase has a card that does it.

62473917
Will Saylor sponsor a team, in Miami maybe?

62475220
>>62473816
Don't trust

62475407
>>62475220
Don't trust what? Coinbase? Just transfer your coin to whatever other wallet you have if you don't trust it.

62477628
>>62475407
I hate coinbase

item.title: I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. — 201 replies — 267.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job.

Open thread • Replies: 201 • Updated: 2026-07-13 20:39 UTC

OP

I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. I'm not young, 40 next year, and don't have a job history or references. High school graduate.
How fucked am I?

Early replies (first 3)

62449979
I live in California, too.

62449982
What did you do for the last 23 years? Hard to help without more context. Do you have skills?

62449989
>>62449982
Depression and NEET means not in education employment or training.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62473155
>>62449961
I bet you were one of those neet posters and now mommy and daddy are getting to old or are dead/dying and you suddenly have to get that wagie job you were making fun of

62473189
>>62473155
>t. wagie
Kek. I am neet and also 40

62473197
>>62473155
Seething wagie, i live toyally of NEETbux + crypto. Tick tock, wagefuck.

62473199
>>62473155
Being a slave isn't a good thing. You're supposed to get out of a bad situation not build a personality around it and defend like a vegan feminist.

62473272
>>62473197
You the VA guy?

62473749
>>62473272
It’s the VA guy

62473786
Well, nevermind that shit. I just went to that store and saw who they hired instead of me. I don't know why I bothered.

62473888
It's the same shit as before, nobody even looks at my applications, nobody even talks to me.

62474038
>>62473888
you decided to look for jobs at the worst time, you retarded nigger

62474334
What are you supposed to do? Nothing you're told to do works.

62474529
>>62449961
just go sell cars. just go into sales. doesnt matter what

62474626
>>62451403
im in the same boat as you OP. start using your local resources. you said you live in the woods? start selling firewood. grow a bunch of plants and start a nursery and start selling plants. designer plants for decoration like japanese maples and shit. its a good side gig. make a website and shit for it then in a year or two when you apply for a real job say you were an entreprenuer and sold your business and want the security net of a regular 9-5 job now with the shitty economy. you can also just backdate this shit and fabricate a website and reviews for something that never happened too btw if you want to just start applying now withou getting any extra money or learning how business works

62474732
>>62450440
They don’t hire anyone retard

62474735
>>62452288
They
Don’t
Hire
You

62474751
even though I'm not a neet after being laid off I feel like I'm in a similar situation.
I have a leg up with my work experience and education but I don't feel like it was worth it which meant that I wasted my youth doing something even worse than just fucking around

62474795
to all the people in this situation who want actual sane advice, here it:

Do something easy. Something that won't demand a superior work ethic or a miserable grind with a manager looking at production numbers watching you.
You're likely gonna hate your life every single morning you wake up and have to work AND you didn't do this when you were 20 years old and it was easy. You can get over the hump and become a "worker" but the mornings are already your enemy, don't stack the odds further against you.

Work behind a register. Ideally at a gas station or some sleepy business. Maybe a long shot for you to get hired, but it's ideal. Some cleaning, some dealing with dickheads, some stocking. You won't be worked to the bone. A grocery/dollar store cashier is worse in most cases.

Inbound call centre. Yes you will hate your job but the work comes at you and your office should be air conditioned. If you have superior (compared to your coworkers) manners and people skills you may even find yourself promoted rapidly. 60 year old grannies get jobs in call centers and end up trainers and managers, you can do it.

Janitor. This one may be a stretch for a 40yo NEET but if you get the right gig, or hop into the right gig after getting a year experience, you may have found a cozy job. Many janitors are not running around like rats and barely have to deal with coworkers or the public. Maybe you end up hired by the local school board in half a year and make "decent" money.


If you really want to try your hand at job where you have a taskmaster watching you try these:

Warehouse worker.
Meatpacker.
Day laborer: Construction, landscaping, whatever.
Factory as a production worker. GL getting hired unless the job is literal hell with high turnover.
Outbound Call Centre

Good luck. If you show up every day for a 1 year or 2 a manager or senior coworker will likely give you a glowing reference. You can even become a real "worker" and have easier mornings in time.

62474821
>>62474795
None of those are hiring.

62474824
>>62474821
Then keep whining here like a bitch. Someone else will take the advice and make a few dollars. If a call centre won't take you, you may as well start picking up cans and bottles.

62474838
>>62474824
Just admit that you're wrong. There are no jobs.

62474844
>>62474795
if you're a man you don't exist. don't go to die in a dead end gas station. karma is real.

62474884
>>62449961
Anon, try asking an LLM (I recommend chat gpt but use whatever) on how to get a job. Explain your entire situation in as much detail as possible, why you haven't worked, depression, you're 40, never had a job, where you live (so it can recommend options) that you're carless.

Explain EVERYTHING. Learning how to utilize LLMs will really help you anyway. I'm getting close to 40 myself and I use LLMs daily (I am a professional with a good job not a NEET but it doesn't matter you need to use LLMs now even if you're employed). Anons ITT are mostly going to dunk on you because you're a huge fucking loser but the best time to turn your life around is right the fuck now. The LLM won't make you feel bad like everyone here is doing too.

Explain your interests to it too and maybe it can try and recommend a job you'll actually excel somewhat in, although beggars can't be choosy at this point.

62474892
>>62474884
the creature making the OP does not want advice. It's doing an act for (you)s
This is actually good advice to someone so defeated they can't find jobs felons on the sex offender registry get after 15 years binned up.
I'm sure a free AI could coach a defeated NEET into one of those jobs.
OP isn't though, just a weird fuck

62475051
>>62474884
I think this is the first time I've seen someone call Ai like chat gpt an "LLM". Well, I guess I've got nothing better to do.

>>62474892
Get a life you fucking loser. Dogshit like you doesn't deserve even the attention I've given you already.

62475932
>>62475051
>Well, I guess I've got nothing better to do.
Repeating myself, but the modern human needs to get comfortable using LLMs to be productive and successful. Avoid falling in to emotional or political traps involving them. View them as a useful tool. A human who refuses to use a calculator would be less productive than a human who does use a calculator.

The same is true for LLMs. Don't blindly trust everything it tells you. Get a feel for how to prompt it to give you the results you want and double check things that you aren't sure about. A lot of people on /biz/ and in general seethe over LLMs, call you a pajeet if you mention them etc, just ignore them. I'm a professional making 6 figs a year in an white collar position, I use LLMs every single day outside of work and inside of work.

As a skill-less NEET who doesn't know how to navigate the world it's the most invaluable tool at your disposal.

62475990
>>62449961
very white european of you

62476030
>>62475932
You put way too much faith in an algorithm poorly masquerading as ai. If it's a tool it should be a learning tool, not a crutch.

62476048
>>62476030
Nowhere did I say it's a crutch, I very clearly said it's a tool. You're starting to sound like you're beyond helping desu. Good luck.

62476501
>>62453688
The fact that you think there is any significant amount of “attacks” in this thread and not genuine criticism shows how far removed you are from the world. It’s going to be a tough couple years for you as you enter the workforce. You’ve received tons of honest advice.

62476747
>>62457849
Anon can we talk on private? I think I can help you with this job for a fraction of 5% of what you get paid. I am desperately in need of money right now.

62477511
OP is a mentally ill retard who is in denial about everything, or maybe just a troll.

ignore this thread.

item.title: Any advice for going to college for Accounting at the ripe age of 26? — 41 replies — 164.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Any advice for going to college for Accounting at the ripe age of 26?

Open thread • Replies: 41 • Updated: 2026-07-13 21:24 UTC

OP

Any advice for going to college for Accounting at the ripe age of 26? I used to be a NEET and then a wagie at a supermarket.

Early replies (first 3)

62475219
>>62475211
Dnr but would pump many a white baby out of that semen demen wemen

62475234
College is great for hot chick, tinder is a gooner dump site.

62475248
>>62475211
I heard that if you are in the US you can get into some accounting positions
with just a cpa loicense which you get through some expedited programs but Im not sure.
Look up some Clarey's vids he has a hard on for accounting and has always recommended it.

If you can get in without a degree than you should do that, you can get a degree on the side
through online programs while you are actually working.

If you have to get a degree than again - study online, don't waste time and money going to a brick and mortar
place it aint worth it.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62475410
>>62475211
Claude fable will decimate accounting job market. Retard

62475424
>>62475410
What’s the safest major in the age of AI sloppa?

62475427
>>62475410
i'm a senior manager at a fortune 10 company. we recently had an AI demo week, showcasing claude and other tools. they all sucked and were a complete joke. what do you do for a living?

62475451
>>62475211
At this point pick up trades and become an electrician. Decent pay and assured source of income for decades.
>>62475427
This sounds like the people that laughted at genAI back in 2023 because it didnt know how to draw hands. It's shortsighted, and while it may very well plateau, just the possibility of it improving exponentially warrants some concerns, specially considering OP is deciding his professional trajectory for the next 35-40 years or so.

62475468
>>62475451
you're assuming that LLMs will overcome their inherent limitations concerning hallucinations or numerical calculation ability.

if all white collar work is at risk of LLM destruction (it's not), then why would anyone pursue a career as an electrician? who would be paying for your services as an electrician?

62475494
>>62475211
Learn the phrase "the combo or just the sandwich?"
Remember that no one asks "would you like fries with that?" ever since the combo meal deal was introduced.

Between accounting databases and AI, expect to be saying that phrase a lot after you graduate.

62475507
>>62475468
I am not assuming anything, I'm just saying that the POSSIBILITY of that technology being able to overcome such limits or at least improving itself enough to make OP field obsolete warrants him considering his options, not straight up discarding accounting.
About the electrician, it's a personal suggestion. Everything runs on electricity, houses will always need repairs, installations and maintenance of that kind, for example. If I could go back in time I would have picked that trade.

62475605
>>62475211
Wow she's cute, what an angle. nw needed to coordinate a careful approach towards her?

62475644
>>62475427
>>62475451
The thing about AI is it is useful, but even in its current iteration it has fundamental flaws that mean for specific jobs it can never do as good as an experienced human. Inexperienced people using AI will do better than if they didn't in many cases, but anybody with decent experience sees the output and sees all of the things wrong with it.
As someone put it, people think AI makes good charts or slides or reports, and then the people who are really good at making charts or slides or reports all say "No that looks bad".

I'm a software engineer and I use a decent amount of AI and had it do great for me, but there's still a number of problems I've thrown at it and it produced total unworkable trash. The thing is, if I was inexperienced I would think it looked good. As someone with 15 years of experience though, I could see its solution would break down rapidly with any other alterations we needed to make.
The experienced human in the loop is still required for anything beyond the absolute basic. As someone who is also in the financial space, AI is absolutely not going to be capable of keeping all the ducks of accounting and finance laws in a row versus an experienced human being.

62475661
>>62475644
best case scenario is AI performs like a retarded offshore indian "senior analyst" who actually performs at the level of a shitty onshore american staff accountant. the offshore indian costs $15k a year and, despite all of his defects, once you learn his personality you can kind of trust him, within limits. the AI will always be prone to confidently lying, it has no permanence or personality so you can never trust it, and the token spend on recreating an offshore indian IQ is $20k a year. so what's the point?

62475708
>>62475211
26 not that old. Older sure, but youre on the edge of being fine.
Just be cool. In all situations people will want to associate with you if you improve their life experience.
Also be serious & career oriented. Youre on the tail end of when fucking around is acceptable.

62475719
>>62475424
Healthcare and accounting

62475722
>>62475211

YOU

JUST

KNOW

62475737
>>62475719
the media is talking about the trades now
in about 10 years it'll be this

so you literally have the next 2-3 years to get your foot in the door in one of these fields where you can still last long enough to have a fulfilling career

62475745
>>62475722
It's almost guaranteed in the year 2026

62475849
>>62475719
>>62475737
Won't demand for healthcare decrease as the baby boomers die off? So if you're 5-10 years away from getting into medicine, you'll most likely enter the job market just as demand starts to take a heavy dip

62476033
>>62475849
we're not going to see a population decline and there is still a huge demand now for all healthcare roles. maybe less geriatric care and more plastics and specialist groups

62476056
>>62475315
That was because you still needed specialized knowledge. Now yiu just ask AI to use the tax codes for you.

62476101
>>62476056
an AI doesn't have the contextual knowledge to appropriately apply tax codes. it's similar to a layman looking up tax codes yet being fined and penalized by the IRS for misunderstanding and fucking things up.

62476748
>>62475719
with robotics and ai those two things are finished in 5-10 years.

62476768
Is it too late to do this at 35?

62476777
>>62475410
>dude just pay 500 dollars for 100 dollars of human labor

62477040
>>62476748
There aren't yet robots good enough to replace manual labor yet, and even besides that it still may require a revolution in energy technology. Your timeline is too immediate. Even for desk jobs, it will probably just mean the low performers lose their jobs and the high performers are expected to do more work (because of/with AI). Maybe models will improve to the point they can truly replace people completely, but I doubt it. They'll still need people to sign off on things as well because the AI companies would go bankrupt if they were held liable for the AI mistakes.

62477168
>>62475211
Are you fucking crazy?

62477217
>>62475234
Yeah..its so great wasting your time at school and studying and getting banged and then you graduate post-wall. lmao. Life is shit for females after 22.

62477250
>>62477217
Beats being an virgin incel in his late 20s like I am. Women can get men wanting them into their 40s and 50s. Men are lucky if one woman wants to have sex with them.

62477525
>>62477250
Not from the men they want lol.
Thats not how this works.
Attractive men are always desirable. Men can be attractive at any age.
If you’re not attractive, then you’re gonna need to beta bucks/provide.
When a girl turns 22+, she is reduced to a cum bucket to the men she actually wants more than just a sex romp with.
That’s the difference.

62477669
>>62477525
Can you make the psy op a little more realistic?

62477691
>>62475424
boob inspector

62477713
>>62477669
What psy op?
Attractive men can be attractive at any age. If you’re not physically attractive, you’re going to need to betabucks.
Girls are only attractive to around age 22.

item.title: OH NO NO NO — 57 replies — 128.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
OH NO NO NO

Open thread • Replies: 57 • Updated: 2026-07-13 20:57 UTC

OP

OH NO NO NO

Early replies (first 3)

62470823
>>62470796
Lol
Lmao even

62470829
>>62470796
amazing it still hasnt fallen 50% oh wait thats' because 4% of the total supply is available. my mistake.

62470830
still above the ipo price. not bad.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62471765
>>62471760
>literal riots
r/wallstreetbets won't do shit

62472184
>>62471090
>SPCX is a good business (Starlink)
Starlink is a cool idea, in the relative Musk Universe of Flying (& Exploding) Horseshit. But even then, you start scratching the surface and it falls apart. It 'makes money' because the fraudulent fat fuck needed at least one co in SPCX portfolio of space garbage that looked like it did. irl, its launch costs are hugely subsidized, those sats have a relatively short shelf life and obv. servicing logistic problematic, system gets worse more customers it has and would fall over at scale, yadda. Its an expensive argument against gravity. And this supposedly SPCX golden child cash cow.

62472192
>>62471760
hope they are mowed down by the national guard, the average iq would improve a lot

62472195
>>62472184
Starlink is also very niche when you think about it. We already have 5G coverage in most of the civilized world. Where it is used, can be considered niche areas like for example Ukrainian drones, airplanes, cruise ships and poor rural places that dont have internet today. How much of a market is that really?

62472196
ATH was $200. Think it'll return to that level in our lifetime?

62472199
>>62472196
LOL no

or may be in 2030

62472271
>>62470796
I'm still baffled by how retarded 90% of /biz/ is. Then I remember most of the oldfag White /biz/ users are gone and this place is 90% jeets. Give it 30 years and MAYBE some of you retards will start to grasp basic economics and financial markets. It really is like your brains are incapable of logic based thinking, even when you've been exposed to the correct information for over a decade. Instead you keep posting the same threads over and over again, thinking you're disseminating some kind of profound knowledge.

62472285
>>62470916
No refunds h*manoyim

62472320
>>62472271
thats a lot of words that say absolutely nothing

62472376
>>62471124
I'll buy when i can have a grok-powered harem of cute catgirls (some catboys too) waifus sucking my cock and doing my house chores 24/7

62472383
>>62472184
>its launch costs are hugely subsidized
>t. literal retard
SpaceX prices its launches far below any other launch provider. They've taken over the entire industry, aside from Chinese/Russian military shit, the Eurotards trying to remain relevant with their three Ariane launches per year, and a few very small players that are desperately trying to horn in (like Blew-Up Origin and Rocket Lab).

Once Super Heavy survives testing and fixing, launch costs are expected to drop by well over another order of magnitude -- some are saying $200/kg to orbit, which means that even ur mom could afford to go (assuming they could cram her inside the fairing).

62472392
>>62472383
>blabla
>entirely ignores actual fucking point he is claiming to respond to
>Moar! Musk PR waffle
>unfunny your Mom 'joke'
isn't it time for your k-doze, fatboi?

62472429
>>62470796
When is the next big unlock? I want to buy puts.

62472445
>>62472320
Yes, if you have an IQ below the White average, you'll struggle with understanding what I just texted. Thanks for making my point.

62472495
I said this shit was going to 135 from the beginning and you baggies called me retarded. Imagine not selling in the first 2 or 3 days after the IPO kek

62472544
>>62470796
>-4%
kek do stocktards really?

62472735
>>62472544
Yeah, that's the kind of daily volatility people expect from a Musk company

62472968
>>62470796
I planned to buy on release and sell the first bump up but didn't have cash available at the time. Shame. I played another meme IPO the exact same way in the past as I was just entering the stock market and it had a very similar pattern.

62474064
>>62472445
no one in this entire world understands you. you are alone.

62475040
>>62470796
waiting for a year after IPO to buy in because Im not stupid and know that we are still in the early adoption phase in terms of what the company is capable of.

62475043
>>62470796
When the spinning ball-Earth is finally exposed worldwide for the 400+ year deception it was, Earth's entire population will suddenly be faced with the reality that every government, every space agency, university, secret society, religious organization, mainstream and alternative media outlet have ALL been duplicitous in propping up a monstrous manipulation to fleece and control the masses. The resulting mass mental exodus away from the control system is exactly what humanity needs. Once the flat Earth truth gets out, these lying politicians, spokesmen, reporters and teachers suddenly change from being heralded voices of authority to being ridiculed, shunned and denounced as they deserve. Once the flat Earth truth gets out, these governments, universities, media outlets and other entangled organizations which have long been hard at work weaving this multi-generational ball-Earth myth, suddenly and completely lose all credibility. Once the truth of our flat Earth gets out, so does the truth of these few elite families/societies who have kept this most important and fundamental reality from us for these hundreds of years! Essentially, once the flat Earth truth gets out, so does every other important truth by proxy, because this "mother-of-all-conspiracies" holds under its umbrella literally ALL of the other conspiracies, and exposes them.

62475117
>>62470796
wheres the worst possible place you can put a rouge ai?
>where you cant fucking unplug it, it has millions of ways to connect to everything on earth that is electronic and distributed over wide area all over our planet.
this is profitable.

62475330
just wait until shares start to unlock, the price is going to fucking tank

62475669
still about $135 more valuable than it should be

62475697
>>62470830
>>62470968
>>62471053

62475706
>>62470796
>tons of people own stock prior to IPO
>no significant new developments to warrant price increases
The only people who didn't expect this outcome are those who didn't do their own research

62475724
No professional money wants anything to do with this garbage. Literally the dumbest retail sheep being sheared right now.

62475739
>>62470954

62475781
State stock meant to fleece the public like trump tokens

62477609
>>62475781
If anyone thought that Trump coin was meant to be any different from the other meme coins they deserved to get dumped on

item.title: >sitting at my WFH job, watching Clavicular clips >go for an hour long walk at lunch time It's fucking insane that normi… — 21 replies — 69.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
>sitting at my WFH job, watching Clavicular clips >go for an hour long walk at lunch time It's fucking insane that normi…

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-07-13 21:06 UTC

OP

>sitting at my WFH job, watching Clavicular clips
>go for an hour long walk at lunch time

It's fucking insane that normies expect me to put the slightest shred of effort in to a job when I'm subhuman.

Early replies (first 3)

62476268
>>62476206
All wagies are cucks because they break their backs while male pornstars and chads have sex with milfs all day.

Quiet quitting is the new meta: why should I, an incel, work when others are paid to fuck? Burn it all to the fucking ground.

62476343
>>62476268
based

62476346
>>62476206
>duuuuuuude why isn’t my goyslop still whimsical!!!!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62476380
>>62476268
Funny what porn does to a man’s mind.

62476397
>>62476380
Who gives a fuck. You seem like someone that's OK with waging while other men fuck. Tell me how that is not more cucked.

62476401
>>62476397
There’s more to life than sex.

62476405
>>62476380
True. I’m incapable of any kind of a relationship with a woman bc I all I see/think about is fucking her. Ironically this makes me way more awkward and insecure bc I want to fuck her and there’s no quicker way on earth to give a woman the ick than to be awkwardly sexually attracted to her. I need to detach myself from the desire of lust. Step one is to stop looking at porn and stop jacking off.

It’s funny bc back in elementary school before I learned that I wanted to fuck girls, but still liked girls, I was able to easily get every girl I liked to like me back simply because I was just treating them like someone I wanted to be good friends with.

62476406
>>62476268
we both spend 8 hours a day on a computer at home. the difference is that i get paid $155k a year to do so. the other difference is that i go outside once i hit 8 hours.

62476412
>>62476406
Inb4 seething replies

62476576
>>62476401
Saying that to an incel is psychopathic. Fucky you, normie.

62476581
>>62476406
And I hope you get beaten by niggers the moment you step outside your door.

62476590
>>62476405
I hope there is a cure to this curse.

62476627
>>62476405
As an incel, it's always a mind fuck to me.
Act uninterested? 95% of the time they simply ignore you as well or 5% of the time you end up friend zoned if you have proximity (At work, friend of a friend, etc).
Act interested? Eww ICK, too forward, pervert, rejection, "I don't just want one thing!", etc.

62476708
>>62476627
The hint it to just be attractive

62476722
>>62476581
why are you so mad?

62477265
>>62476722
Why were you born a cuck

62477277
>>62476406

62477284
>>62476268
Sage

62477602
>>62477277
senior manager, financial reporting/accounting

62477623
>>62476406
i would not even get out of my bed before 11AM for 155K gross

62477642
>>62477623

62475556

62476227

62476239

62476359

item.title: memes aside, is LINK in an accumulation zone below $8? — 9 replies — 10.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
memes aside, is LINK in an accumulation zone below $8?

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-07-13 20:31 UTC

OP

memes aside, is LINK in an accumulation zone below $8?

Early replies (first 3)

62475926
>>62475875
Token doesn't have value in long term so no.

62475929
p+c

62476525
>>62475875
it's going to $4 by december and it won't clear $20 in the next cycle

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62476532
if you want to accumulate poop, sure

62476558
I'm buying at $3 later this year

62476560
>>62475875
Memes aside?
Probably, maybe.

Realistically yes, all crypto is in an accumulation zone right now

62476582
>>62475875
All of crypto is in accumulation zone right now. The only reason I’m not buying is my bags were loaded up years ago, and I’m buying other things

62476591
Every time LINK goes low I really want to accumulate but I never do, and then a year later I'm always really, really glad that I didn't. So I'll just stick to that strategy

62477482
>>62475875
No. Unironically maybe below $2 which is where revenues actually make sense vs. subsidized network bloat. Still would not buy; there is zero actual source of demand no matter how successful CLL ever become.

item.title: Do you think SPCX IPO price ($135) will be some sort of "floor"? — 4 replies — 8.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Do you think SPCX IPO price ($135) will be some sort of "floor"?

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-07-13 20:55 UTC

OP

Do you think SPCX IPO price ($135) will be some sort of "floor"? On one hand, it's the IPO price. On the other hand, it's not a round number support like 140 or 130 and Given the war with Iran, oil prices, and the overall market trend, it doesn't look very good.

Early replies (first 3)

62477507
>>62477492
There are another three Chinese Falcon 9 clones that are likely to perform a successful landing over the next 6 months, and about a dozen Chinese Falcon 9 clones over the next 30 months.

62477533
ain't no floors in space Son
you've entered The New Dimension of Bottomless Trash now.

62477554
>>62477507
He can't compete with chinese EVs, he can't compete with chinese robotics, he can't compete even with chinese AI, and now he won't be able to compete with chinese rockets. I hope he goes bankrupt and kills himself.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62477592

People that bought the pedo con man state funded company stocks have frontal lobe damage.

item.title: both Swift and DTCC are going live right now — 118 replies — 135.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
both Swift and DTCC are going live right now

Open thread • Replies: 118 • Updated: 2026-07-13 20:32 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62466455
Price?

62466457
>>62466455
Suppressed,

62466461
And literal top-buying turned full time fudders are finally capitulating.

Imagine subjecting yourself to all the pain and never seeing it through the end. I gave myself 6 months after SWIFT and DTCC are live to see if the token shoots up, and then decide wether to sell half my stack of just staking rewards.

But to sell RIGHT BEFORE the literal ANNOUNCED DATES of the two biggest financial behemoths' launches? That's crazy

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62472062
>>62471956
lol

That's literally what you said though:
>>62466708
>But link that is used to pay nodes doesn't come from the market. Which has been the main problem of why link is disassociated from the network.

>>62471975
See >>62466927

62472069
>>62466457
This will keep happening. Link won't re-enter the top 5 until btc goes to 35k

62472415
>>62472062
I swear we had this conversation before. The BTC network is inflationary but all new BTC that enters the market comes from the miners and they're the ones who put a price tag on it. They'll most definitely factor in the cost of operations into the coin. New Link that enters circulation comes not only from node operators selling but Chainlink Labs themselves selling tokens. More importantly, access to the node operators is supposed to be gatekept by Link tokens, but CLL keeps giving people outs to access the network without buying the Link token by paying in other currencies and CLL sits on their laurels and decides when and how much off their off chain revenue should be converted to Link from the markets ($1M a week). Stop being dishonest about the Link token.

62472614
>>62472415
>miners decide the price of BTC
You're legitimately retarded.

62472624
>>62472415
>CLL keeps giving people outs to access the network without buying the Link token
If users don't have to "buy" the Link token, they still have to "pay for" the token.
Just like if you pay for a package delivery, you're not "buying" the gasoline for the delivery truck, but you are paying for it.

62472630
>>62472062
>>62466927
>Some anon did the math in another thread;
Link to thread?

62472819
>>62467960
Obviously I meant for us to track it

62472871
>>62472614
Point exactly what I said about BTC and plug both it and your reinterpretation into ChatGPT.

>>62472624
>Just like if you pay for a package delivery, you're not "buying" the gasoline for the delivery truck, but you are paying for it.
The native token fees for CCIP are sitting in a contract and have yet to be converted to Link (PA version 2 is said to automate this). CLL converts offchain revenue at $1M a week where we have no idea what percentage of this is being converted in their offchain revenue.

You are theoretically correct about having to cover the costs of gas when buying X at the grocery store but in this case, not even the delivery trucks are buying gas with the payments made. The delivery trucks' management company has a gas reserve to fill up their tanks and they're not replenishing the gas reserve from gas on the market. Hence why the market price of gas isn't going up.

62472938
>>62472871
>exactly what I said about BTC
Here's exactly what you said: "all new BTC that enters the market comes from the miners and they're the ones who put a price tag on it"

You're a fucking retard who thinks miners determine the price of BTC.

>The delivery trucks' management company has a gas reserve to fill up their tanks and they're not replenishing the gas reserve from gas on the market.
Holy shit yes they are.

62473105
>>62472871
>The delivery trucks' management company has a gas reserve to fill up their tanks and they're not replenishing the gas reserve from gas on the market.
yeah this is definitely wrong lmao

62473264
>>62473105
That's exactly how all crypto inflationary rewards work.
For example: 95% of Bitcoin mining rewards are inflationary, and only like 5% have to be bought by users from the open market.

And in the case of Link, much of it is being bought on the open market via payment abstraction.

You cripples really need to learn how to sing a new tune.

62473278
>>62473264
No wonder you get grifted.
You dont understand supply and demand, how price is determined and that btc "inflation" =/= 8% supply increase aka "strategic unlocks"

62473280
>>62473278
No, you don't understand those things.

62474448
>>62472938
And what did I say after that?
>>62473105
>Holy shit yes they are.
>yeah this is definitely wrong lmao
What, the $1M a week Reserve buyback? The CCIP fee tokens stuck in a contract that have yet to be converted to Link? Realize when you give these fucking bullshit "technically..." answers, if you were right, then you would need to contend to why the price action is fucking shit. Why Chainlink is realizing the goals it was aiming to achieve for over 10 years in the release of DTCC's and SWIFT's DLT platforms and yet the price is $8. Why Chainlink is onboarding over $7B in protocol value to CCIP and the price is still $8. Hundreds of millions of dollars of revenue generated and still $8.

And notice you never directly addressed my points about $LINK being decoupled from the network. You keep making inaccurate comparisons.

62474875
>>62474448
>And what did I say after that?
After that you said "They'll most definitely factor in the cost of operations into the coin".
Which further proves that you thought miners determined the price of Bitcoin.

You're such a massive retard lmao.

>if you were right, then you would need to contend to why the price action is fucking shit
Two reasons:
1) crypto adoption as a whole is still insignificant, and all crypto valuation so far is speculative instead of functional
2) this >>62466457 >>62467657 >>62468805

62474886
>>62467048
whats stopping them from being paid in link then immediately selling that link for something else

62474889
>>62474886
lmao what's stopping literally any crypto miner/node on any L1 from doing that?

Why do link fuddies always have to be so explicit about being absolutely clueless on crypto?

62474913
>>62474448
well, i'll give you this pal, i did read, but i am, in fact, never selling.

62476934
>>62474889
because mined crypto has intrinsic value of the energy spent to create it, or revenue share or buybacks.

62476944
>>62476934
>mined crypto has intrinsic value of the energy spent to create it
please tell me you're only joking

62477003
This idea that link/xrp cucks have that banks will all of a sudden start buying mass amount of their respective shitcoins is truly a fascinating mass psychoses to behold. This board is basically an insane asylum at this point between the xrp and link spam.

62477008
>>62476944
the market must be a comedian too because that joke is told in every link pair chart

62477021
>>62477003
If banks want to use Chainlink (and they do), they have to pay for the work Chainlink nodes do, which is denominated and paid exclusively in Link tokens.

62477094
>>62474875
>After that you said "They'll most definitely factor in the cost of operations into the coin".
>Which further proves that you thought miners determined the price of Bitcoin.

Anon, you're calling me a retard for saying a miner, someone running operations would sell bitcoins at a loss. What happens to a business that doesn't make a profit? What do you think goes into a the mind of a miner received BTC out of the coin base and is about it sell it. You think he's gonna say "hmm, I rely on this to keep my operations running, I should sell it for lower than what it takes to keep the lights running"?

>>62474875
>1) crypto adoption as a whole is still insignificant, and all crypto valuation so far is speculative instead of functional
But Chainlink's revenue is far from insignificant and they gloat about generating hundreds of millions of dollars in revenue generated. Link has always been suppressed. However, can you show that $Link has been battling it out and contributing to fighting against the suppression in contributing to its price? For reasons I previously mentioned multiple times that you refuse to directly address, CLL is sabotaging its own internet funny money by giving customers like myself an avenue to bypass it and pay in USD. And only a meager and unknown percentage has been traceably converted to Link between the Reserve, SVR, and CCIP.

>>62474913
no conflicts between not selling and being realistic about the current tokenonmics till staking releases.

62477138
>>62477021
Its a flat fee in dollars, converted to link, paid to node operators, sold for dollars. Its an unneeded hop.

62477152
>>62477094
You didn't say "miners don't want to sell at a loss", you said "miners are the ones who put a price tag on BTC".

>>62477138
>Its an unneeded hop
It really isn't. For the simplicity and uniformity of the basic infrastructure alone it makes perfect sense to use a single token.
And even notorious Link fudder Vitalik admits the economic (dis)incentive aspect makes a token needed as well.

62477191
>>62477152
>And even notorious Link fudder Vitalik
He is jelly , he fiddled with the whole proof of work thing while he could have printed out of thin air and just dumped annualy in the form of token unlocks.

The only good thing with the link conversion/sale is the node operators can sell it for thei local jeetcoin currency.

62477445
>>62477152
>You didn't say "miners don't want to sell at a loss", you said "miners are the ones who put a price tag on BTC".

62477487
>>62477445
If you think those are the same thing I can see why you are frustrated with someone pointing out the error to begin with.

The market decides the price. Miners have 2 options:

1. Sell now to cover costs
2. Eat the costs and wait for the market to improve

Neither scenario is them setting the price. Scenario 2 is miners speculating with coins the same way any market participant does, except their tokens were previously uncirculating.

item.title: isnt this kinda sussy baka — 37 replies — 50.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
isnt this kinda sussy baka

Open thread • Replies: 37 • Updated: 2026-07-13 20:40 UTC

OP

why is btc have value if xmr is needed to purchase things on dark web but btc is just a coin and is not needed. xmr is needed and therefore has more value, right? forgive me if im wrong but, is the reason BTC is still alive just because it's the one that got all the attention at the start?

also anons check with ur library to see if theres a morningstar dashboard, might be nice if there is.

btw has anyone here looked into cs2 markets or anything? i know it sounds crazy, but people think crypto is crazy too.

Early replies (first 3)

62468595
>>62468583
Xmr is for midwits to hold and high IQ individuals to use like the utility it is. You wouldn't understand my simpleton friend

62468770
>>62468583
My friend makes money on the side with CS2 market flips. Waits for update news, buys up shit that's cheap but won't be because of some bullshit changes, then just waits. Chinks pump up the prices like crazy and he benefits.

62468973
>>62468583
Because bitcoin's price is based on speculation
if it's price was based on use it wouldn't even be a 100 dollars

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62471297
>>62468583
Because institutions are all-in on btc. Why not XMR? Because Monero doesn't work in the system's favor. The elites don't need to care if all of their transactions are public, they are just using shadow corporations.
So in a sense btc became a stand-in for all cryptos in the minds of normies.

>>62470104
There would literally be no demand for XMR if it primarily was an sov.

62471322
>>62470104
>If Bitcoin was able to get to 126k with just whale collusion and bullshit
It only got to that price because of institutionalization and trump pumping it just to dump. Without that it hit 1/2 that price. XMR isn't even readily available to ameritards due to it's standing. Yet, BTC was always available.
Holy cope of a thread. FPBP

62471336
>>62468595
This. So normies need a larger more robust coin for privacy to lean on. Similar to other markets leaning on property and gold to justify their own worth during crisis.

62471599
>>62468583
xmr will eventually reach five figures by becoming a significant beneficiary of capital flight from the horrors of late-stage statism

62472716
>>62471297
>the law of supply and demand doesn’t apply to Monero if people like it and want to hold it
I’m just so tired of shills/retards/BTC-brainbroken people applying new rules just for Monero. What does it even mean for a coin to be “primarily a SoV”? People use Monero for whatever reason they want. Some use it primarily for transacting (because it works) and some use it primarily for holding (because it works). That’s how a fucking functional currency operates.

>>62471322
>a shitcoin with literally no utility outside of a 21M collection size NFT was available to buy on Coinbase
>this is relevant to Monero
At the end of the day it comes down to whether you actually believe crypto is a real technology or not. Is it possible to create a censorship resistant asset with privacy that actually works as the native internet currency of the world? Because Monero is LITERALLY the only one that even has a chance of becoming that—-the entire point of crypto in the first place. No amount of regulation or liquidity restriction can prevent it from taking hold eventually. The world is far too multipolar now, no single nation can stop it.

62472732
>>62468595
>Makes fun of people who think that holding XMR will achieve anything except making it a new object to gamble on

>>62470104
>Gets mad about positive price development coming from the usage of XMR and not just stacking it because you can't force demand by choking supply

>>62471297
>Explains that even if just over 50% of XMR is used for holding demand would fall rapidly

>>62472716
>Doesn't understand a thing but assumes this is because of the psyop that is modern BTC usage despite it being mainly valued because of it being an sov and institutionalized

62472733
>hasanabi thread full of faggots sucking cock and screaming at each other
You losers are exactly what everyonr thinks of you lmfaooooo

62472759
>>62472732
>supply and demand doesn’t apply to Monero
You can keep pretending this is the case, but it isn’t.

62472781
>>62472759
>Bro people totally want a currency that no one uses

62472786
>>62472732
>bitcoin is valuable because it’s a sov
>if you think Monero would go up if it became more of a sov you’re retarded

>bitcoin has no usecase but it’s valuable people people hold it
>if even 50% of XMR was held, demand would fall rapidly
You’re just a transparent shill faggot. You have no basis for any of this. Why would demand fall when the supply shrinks? Literally economics 101: supply shrinks, price RISES. But the rules change when you replace “BTC” with “XMR”. You’re FULL OF SHIT

62472793
>>62472786
>Guys lets just copy the psyop that has been adopted by all the jewish system
Man, I really overlooked that possibility didn't I?

62472795
>>62468583
There's this thing called speculation, go learn about it.
It pretty much sums up all crypto.

62472796
>>62472793
>holding a coin is Jewish
Literally what the FUCK are you taking about

62472807
>>62472796
>BTC totally hasn't been integrated into the jewish system
Anon, you've already lost. Come back in a month telling us we need more compliance so judenstein can buy gorillions of XMR

62472813
>>62472807
Buy Monero. Hold Monero. That’s all I, OP, or anyone else here says.

You can pretend that means “compliance” but it doesn’t, and it’s telling that you have to blatantly lie just to make your point.

62472830
>>62472813
Again, why would anyone want to get into Monero if all it does is being held? There's no reason. The value of XMR stems from its transactional qualities. Why would I buy your bags? What would you even do with them once you're ready to transact your XMR? Do you just want to give some illiquid good to your kids when you kick the bucket?
>But it works for BTC
Because it's been psyoped and adopted by the jews for that reason

62472843
>>62472830
You’re just flailing around autistically screeching right now. It’s sad. You don’t have to be this way.

62473043
>>62472843
Please tell me why anyone would buy XMR if it's primarily just moonfag's bags

62473108
>>62473043
moe and sov aren't mutually exclusive, false dichotomy

62473123
>>62473108
>There would literally be no demand for XMR if it primarily was an sov. (>>62471297)

Try to keep up

62473376
>>62473123
>see? I said some bullshit before. No I don’t have to prove it. I just said it.

62473396
>>62473376
I already established that if XMR was primarily used as an sov it's demand would drop massively. Fomo only works if the product is attractive due to internal motivation

62473588
>>62473396
What the fuck do you mean you “established it”? You literally just asserted it without the slightest attempt at supporting your argument.

“Monero demand would not drop massively if people held it. In fact that claim violates the most basic principles of economics”

There. I established the counterpoint. What now?
>(am I actually just arguing with an autistic teenager? It seems like it)

62473623
>>62473588
>What the fuck do you mean you “established it”? You literally just asserted it without the slightest attempt at supporting your argument.
The word 'if' signals that an argument makes an assumption. Any discussions derived from that argument which are not attacking the assumption itself presuppose the assumption.
>There. I established the counterpoint.
Your point isn't countering mine. That's because I used the word 'primarily'. This means the assumption of my argument is that over 50% of Monero's 'usage' was solely as an sov. Your point is about any non-zero amount of XMR as an sov.
>In fact that claim violates the most basic principles of economics”
This is wrong. There is simply no reason to buy your bags. If Monero was primarily used as an sov there would be no meaningful difference to a non-KYCed btc wallet. You could argue about fungibility now but not even half the board knows this term so this is a negligible factor.
I also told you before that even if you are about to argue that it worked out for btc you're neglecting
A. btc has now been adopted by the jewish system, Monero is too democratic in its power concentration to have the same happening to it
B. XMR's qualities stem from its transactional qualities so with every hodler added the appeal of XMR shrinks
So if you want to defend your position you now need to show us why people would be interested into entering XMR if it was *primarily* used as an sov. No one is arguing about supply and demand not existing as a framework for finding the value of something, we're arguing about demand existing in actuality under the assumption of XMR mainly being used as an sov.

62474147
>>62473623
>Any discussions derived from that argument which are not attacking the assumption itself presuppose the assumption.
This statement alone should be enough to stop anyone from ever talking to you again.

>>62468583
correct. xmr is the real bitcoin because it has all the satoshi requirements

62474407
>>62473623
>I used the word 'primarily'
So? This whole idea that Monero is “primarily” any particular thing already presupposes your bullshit idea that MoE and SoV are inherently contradictory or adversarial with one another. They’re not.

Monero is not primarily anything. It’s not primarily transactional it’s not primarily store of value. It is whatever people want it to be.

>There is simply no reason to buy your bags.
lol ok weirdo I’m gonna just buy and hold Monero and nobody will ever be able to detect it, tax it, freeze it, trace it, or seize it. But apparently that’s a worthless utility.

>XMR's qualities stem from its transactional qualities
Repeating this doesn’t make it true btw.

62477241
>>62474147

>>62474407

No, I said that because you complained in >>62470104 about different people using XMR differently.
>They’re not.
The value proposition of XMR as an sov derives solely from its qualities of it being an moe. So by you arguing for sov, you're reducing its sov properties.
>It is whatever people want it to be.
But then he cries about >>62468595
>But apparently that’s a worthless utility.
It's only not worthless because people actually use it.
>Repeating this doesn’t make it true btw.
It being true is what makes it true.

62477452
>>62477241
Why wouldn't someone just use the xmr to swap for btc and retain the same level of privacy until they're ready to sell? Fungibility? If that was such a significant issue then there would be absolutely no offers to buy btc on haveno. Monero will always be a useful privacy layer for crypto but it can hardly be considered a hedge against btc so what's the upside?

62477516
>>62477452
A black box system can easily be found out if you can closely watch the input and output. This means you're basically just creating a convoluted btc mixer, even if you use different wallets.
